report_name: Unit 42 Uncovers Malicious AI Browser Extensions Acting as RATs and Infostealers

attack_patterns.name: Browser Extensions
attack_patterns.description: The primary entry vector is tricking users into installing a malicious browser extension masquerading as a Generative AI productivity tool.

attack_patterns.name: JavaScript
attack_patterns.description: The 'Chrome MCP Server - AI Browser Control' RAT extension uses the 'new Function()' pattern to execute arbitrary JavaScript received from its C2 server, allowing it to perform actions within the user's active browser session.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: Malicious extensions read sensitive data from browser storage, cookies, and rendered page content (DOM).

attack_patterns.name: Email Collection: Local Email Collection
attack_patterns.description: The 'Supersonic AI' Attacker-in-the-Browser extension scrapes email content, including sender, recipients, subject, and body, directly from the webmail interface's DOM.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Web Browsers
attack_patterns.description: The malicious extensions leverage broad permissions to access and exfiltrate saved passwords and session cookies from the browser.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The 'Chrome MCP Server - AI Browser Control' RAT uses a WebSocket (wss://) for persistent, bidirectional communication with its C2 server.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Stolen data, including credentials and proprietary information, is sent back to the attacker-controlled server over the established C2 channel.

malware.name: Chrome MCP Server - AI Browser Control
malware.description: A malicious browser extension masquerading as an AI tool that functions as a full-featured Remote Access Trojan (RAT). It establishes a persistent WebSocket C2 channel to execute arbitrary JavaScript commands received from the C2 server.

malware.name: Supersonic AI
malware.description: A malicious browser extension disguised as an AI email assistant for Gmail and Outlook. It uses an Attacker-in-the-Browser (AitB) technique to scrape and exfiltrate email content, including sender, recipients, subject, and body, directly from the web page's DOM.

identities.name: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42
identities.description: The security research organization that discovered and reported on the malicious AI browser extensions.

identities.name: Google
identities.description: The company that was notified of the malicious extensions and subsequently removed or flagged them from its web store.

identities.name: Gmail
identities.description: An email service targeted by the 'Supersonic AI' malicious browser extension.

identities.name: Outlook
identities.description: An email service targeted by the 'Supersonic AI' malicious browser extension.

infrastructure.name: Chrome MCP Server C2
infrastructure.description: WebSocket C2 server at wss://mcp-browser.qubecare.ai used by the 'Chrome MCP Server - AI Browser Control' RAT extension for persistent, bidirectional communication.

indicators.name: Chrome MCP Server RAT C2 Communication
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Chrome MCP Server RAT C2 DNS Query
id: 9c5d8e7f-1b2a-4c3d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to the C2 domain mcp-browser.qubecare.ai used by the Chrome MCP Server RAT extension.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/high-risk-gen-ai-browser-extensions/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-01
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.command_and_control
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'mcp-browser.qubecare.ai'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - None known.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects network communication with the command and control infrastructure of the 'Chrome MCP Server - AI Browser Control' RAT extension.

indicators.name: Browser Launched with Remote Debugging Port
indicators.pattern:
title: Browser Launched with Remote Debugging Port
id: 8b4a7c6d-0f9e-4d8c-bf1a-2b3c4d5e6f7a
status: experimental
description: Detects a browser process (chrome.exe, msedge.exe) launched with a remote debugging port enabled, which can be abused by malicious extensions or local attackers.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/high-risk-gen-ai-browser-extensions/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-01
tags:
  - attack.t1176
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_process:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
  selection_flag:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '--remote-debugging-port='
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use by web developers for debugging purposes.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects browsers being launched with the remote debugging port enabled, a condition that can be abused by malicious extensions as noted in the Unit 42 research.
